title: DISPATCH_RESULT
description: The result of a dispatch operation. Distinguishes between ready_to_dispatch (TaskSpec ready but not yet dispatched) and dispatched (runner has been invoked).

dispatch_status.description: Dispatch state. ready_to_dispatch means a TaskSpec exists but has not been dispatched. dispatched means the runner has been invoked. taskspec_generated means the TaskSpec was created but dispatch is pending. manual_confirm_required means human confirmation is needed. stopped means the flow has terminated. failed means dispatch failed.

next_stage.description: Next stage identifier. Required when dispatch_status is dispatched and should_execute_next is true.

next_task_spec_path.description: Path to the next TaskSpec. Required when dispatch_status is taskspec_generated or ready_to_dispatch.

required_next_action.description: Concrete next action. Required when dispatch_status is manual_confirm_required, stopped, or failed.

resume_command.description: The command to resume execution. Required when dispatch_status is stopped or manual_confirm_required, and terminal is false.

terminal.description: Is this explicit bounded dispatch chain terminal? If false, the automation must continue the declared chain; it must not invent a later project milestone.

should_execute_next.description: Should the next stage or TaskSpec be executed? False for stopped, manual_confirm_required, or failed.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the dispatch status.
